description: Use when choosing which Claude Code tool fits a task (Read/Edit/Write, Bash, grep, Agent, plan mode, TaskCreate, WebFetch, MCP, slash commands), debugging "should this be Bash or a dedicated tool", deciding when to background a long-running command, picking between grep and Agent(Explore) for a search, or auditing loaded MCPs for cost. Routes to chapter 15 (tool palette) of claude-spine.

results.query: should this be a Bash call or one of the dedicated tools? I want to count lines in 5 files and write the result to a report
the build tool keeps complaining about missing types in tsconfig — fix the paths config
should I use the Edit tool or the Write tool to update this 60-line file — only changing 4 lines but they're not contiguous?
the CI tool is failing on the linting step — debug the github action
implement the file-upload tool in the dashboard — accept PDFs up to 10MB
this `npm run build` is going to take 8 minutes — should I background it with run_in_background, or is there a smarter pattern?
I want to search the whole codebase for every place that calls 'createServerClient' — is grep the right move or should I spawn an Explore agent?
I've got 11 MCPs loaded and my context bar is full before I even start — which ones are pulling their weight and which should I unload?
design the tools page of the marketing site — three columns with feature cards
what's the right ORM tool for a Postgres + Next.js stack in 2026
